name: Cable Crunch
resistance_profile: Loads spinal flexion with progressive resistance — trains the rectus abdominis for growth.
rom_notes: Flex the spine to crunch the ribs toward the pelvis; control back to a stretch.
cues: Fixed hips; move only the trunk
execution_steps: Attach a rope to a high pulley, kneel facing the stack, and hold the rope ends beside your head.
Set your hips back slightly and keep them fixed in place for the entire set.
Crunch by rounding your spine, driving your ribs down toward your pelvis.
Squeeze the abs hard at the bottom, then let the weight slowly pull you back up to a stretch.
good_when: You want to load the abs with progressive resistance for growth by adding weight over time.
You already do bodyweight crunches easily and need more resistance.
You have a cable stack and want a joint-friendly ab isolation.
bad_when: No cable machine is available (use a weighted crunch instead).
You want to train anti-rotation or bracing (use a plank or Pallof press).
You keep turning it into a hip-hinge and can't feel the abs (a lying crunch may be easier to learn on).
common_errors: Turning it into a hip-flexor pull
Using the arms to pull the weight
